product_surfaces: internal/credential Pipeline.WithProbe() auth probe failure class
CLI auth status command via config.RenderCredentialStatus()
evidence_type: offline executable runtime validation through Go tests generated by run.sh and direct CLI invocation
freshness: current repository checkout; validation exercises production internal/credential package behavior at unit level and CLI auth status command at integration level

mocks_used.name: memSource
mocks_used.purpose: provide deterministic GITCODE_TOKEN values without reading operator environment variables

mocks_used.name: probe callback
mocks_used.purpose: simulate auth probe success/failure without network access

mocks_used.name: testAuthFailureError
mocks_used.purpose: test ClassifyAuthError without depending on real gitcode error types

product_gaps.description: The credential.Pipeline with AuthStatus.RenderText() correctly produces redacted token output and token validation diagnostics, but the CLI's auth status command at internal/cli/cli.go:415-430 still uses config.DefaultCredentialProvider() (the old ChainCredentialProvider path) and config.RenderCredentialStatus(). The config.CredentialStatus struct lacks RedactedToken, TokenDiagnostic, and AuthProbeResult fields. The redacted token information exists in the credential package boundary but is not surfaced to CLI output. The CLI auth status command reports source and token_present but no redacted_token line. The task plan explicitly constrains changes to internal/credential/ only, preventing CLI wiring changes as part of this task.

notes: Live network and real OS keychain access are intentionally disabled for this validation materialization.

Unit-level tests for RedactToken(), ValidateTokenFormat(), ClassifyAuthError(), Pipeline.Status() with probes, and AuthStatus.RenderText() all pass, confirming the credential package correctly implements redacted token reporting and auth-failure diagnostics.

The CLI integration check detects a product gap: the new credential.Pipeline and its AuthStatus.RenderText() are not yet wired into the CLI auth status command path. The CLI still uses config.DefaultCredentialProvider() and config.RenderCredentialStatus(), which do not carry redacted token info.
This gap is expected given the task scope (internal/credential/ only) and should be addressed in a follow-up task that wires credential.Pipeline into the CLI.
